items.q: What does negative feedback do in homeostasis?
items.correct: It reverses a change in a controlled variable, bringing it back towards the set point.
items.misconception: Negative feedback makes a process get worse or amplifies the change.
items.random_wrong: Negative feedback stops the body from responding to any internal change.
items.why: The word 'negative' has bad connotations, so students think it makes things worse, missing that it counteracts deviations.

items.q: What is the role of the guide RNA in CRISPR-Cas9?
items.correct: It directs Cas9 to the specific DNA sequence to cut by base-pairing with the target.
items.misconception: Guide RNA is the new sequence that gets inserted into the genome.
items.random_wrong: Guide RNA is the enzyme that cuts the DNA.
items.why: Students confuse the guide RNA (which targets) with Cas9 (which cuts) and with any donor DNA (which is inserted) — three distinct components.

items.q: Where do plants get most of their biomass from?
items.correct: From carbon dioxide in the air — the carbon in plant tissue comes mostly from CO2 fixed during photosynthesis.
items.misconception: Plants get most of their mass from the soil through their roots.
items.random_wrong: Plants gain most of their mass directly from sunlight, which has weight.
items.why: Roots in soil look like the source of food/mass, but actually most plant dry mass is carbon from atmospheric CO2.

items.q: Xylem transport is best described as:
items.why: It's Passive in dead cells. “Active living transport” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The Casparian strip is located in the:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Endodermis. “Epidermis” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If a tree transpires 200 L/day and leaf SA is 100 m², transpiration rate in L m⁻² day⁻¹ is:
items.why: Exact answer: 2.0. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
